title: Quantifying the historical and future heat-related mortality above the heat alert thresholds of the inaugural Chinese national heat-health action plan

L2: Temperatures above 35C were associated with 0.51% of mortality in China between 2016-2019
Heat-health action plans require evidence-based threshold calibration for effective mortality prevention
L3: Study quantifying historical and projected heat-related mortality burden in China above the heat alert thresholds defined by the 2023 national heat-health action plan, providing epidemiological evidence for climate adaptation policy
